title: Director Reference Library
description: Your quick-access reference for contacts, system paths, billing codes, and key resources. Return to this module anytime you need a fast answer.

sections.title: Vendor & Contact Directory
learningObjectives: Have immediate access to all key vendor and emergency contacts
Know which towing company serves which location
Know the licensing contact and insurance agent
sections.content:

sections.title: Key Links & System Access
learningObjectives: Know the URLs for voicemail management, DSS portal, and VA licensing regulations
Know the Canva login for newsletter and marketing design
Know where to find all key documents in Google Drive
sections.content:

### Design & Communication

**Canva Access (for newsletters, flyers, social content):**
- Login email: mollypetchel@gmail.com
- Password: BBIteach1984!
- The Bright Beginnings brand kit is pre-loaded in this account

---

### Google Drive: Where to Find Key Documents

| Onboarding Documents | Drive → HR → Onboarding |
| Licensing Binder Checklist | Drive → Compliance → Licensing |

> **Access issue?** Contact admin. Do not create a duplicate document — always use the master version in Drive.

sections.title: ProCare Quick Reference
learningObjectives: Find any common ProCare report in 3 clicks or fewer
Know how to enter a payment, run an aging report, and check immunizations
Complete the 10 most common ProCare tasks without searching
sections.content:
## ProCare: 10 Tasks You Do Every Week

This is your cheat sheet. Every path starts from the ProCare main dashboard.

---

### 1. Run the Aging / Bad Debt Report
**Path:** Reports → Financial → Aging Report
- Filters: set date range to current month; sort by balance descending
- Export to PDF and attach to your DOR submission

---

### 2. Enter a Tuition Payment
**Path:** Families → [select family] → Transactions → Add Payment
- Select payment type: Check, Cash, or Credit Card
- Enter amount, date, and check number (if check)
- Print receipt if family requests it

---

### 3. Apply a Late Fee
**Path:** Families → [select family] → Transactions → Add Transaction → Late Fee
- Select the correct fee code for your location
- Enter the date (Day 1 of the month)
- Add a note: "Late fee — [month]"

---

### 4. Run the Roll Call / Attendance Report
**Path:** Reports → Attendance → Roll Call Report
- Filter by classroom and date
- Print and distribute to each room by 8:30 AM

---

### 5. Check Immunization Expirations
**Path:** Reports → Child → Immunization Expiration Report
- Run monthly; filter for expiring within 60 days
- Export list; begin parent contact for any flagged records

---

### 6. Run the Hours Worked / Overtime Report
**Path:** Reports → Payroll → Hours Worked Report
- Run mid-week (Wednesday) to catch overtime risk
- Filter by week; sort by hours descending
- Adjust afternoon schedules for anyone at 35+ hours by Wednesday

---

### 7. Add a New Employee
**Path:** Staff → Add Employee
- Complete all required fields: name, address, SSN (encrypted), hire date, position, rate
- Set supervisor and schedule
- Upload all required documents to the employee record
- Must be complete by Friday 5:00 PM of hire week

---

### 8. Run the Deposit Report
**Path:** Reports → Financial → Deposit Report
- Run on Day 2 of each month for the prior month
- Submit with DOR to admin

---

### 9. Run the Billing Audit Report
**Path:** Reports → Financial → Billing Audit Report
- Run on Day 20 of each month
- Look for: accounts missing enhancement fee, CCA accounts missing ^ symbol, duplicate transactions

---

### 10. Run the Revenue Forecast
**Path:** Reports → Financial → Revenue Forecast
- Run on Day 2 of each month
- Compare to enrollment target; flag gaps in the Director Packet
- Update mid-month if enrollment changes significantly

---

### Quick Billing Reference

| Location | TE Merchant ID | CCA Code |
|----------|----------------|----------|
| Crozet | 7657 | 9252 |
| Forest Lakes | 7660 | 2935 |
| Mill Creek | 7673 | 9304 |

sections.title: Tadpoles Reference
learningObjectives: Know how to check lesson plan submissions in Tadpoles
Know how to print attendance and daily reports
Understand the director review workflow for daily notes
sections.content:
## Tadpoles: Director Functions

Tadpoles is primarily the classroom documentation and parent communication tool — but the director has key review and oversight functions.

---

### Checking Lesson Plan Submissions

**Path:** Admin → Lesson Plans → View Submitted Plans
- Check every Thursday that all lead teachers have submitted plans for the following week
- Plans not submitted by Thursday: contact the teacher directly (not via Group Me)
- Flag missing submissions in the Director Packet

---

### Reviewing Daily Notes

**Path:** Admin → Daily Reports → By Classroom → [Date]
- Review all children's notes daily — your review happens between 3:00 PM and close
- Check for: completeness (2+ sentences), photo quality (2+ staged photos), accuracy
- Staff with incomplete or low-quality notes: brief verbal feedback before they leave for the day; document pattern in ProCare if recurring

---

### Printing Attendance Reports

**Path:** Admin → Attendance → Print Daily Attendance → [Date] → [Classroom]
- Use for licensing binder documentation (monthly)
- Use for any incident where attendance verification is needed
- Can be printed by classroom or by whole-center

---

### Sending the Parent AM Message

**Path:** Messages → Send Group Message → Select All Families → [Compose]
- Must be sent by **9:15 AM daily — no exceptions**
- Keep to 2–4 sentences; use the template guide in Google Drive
- Topics rotate: curriculum update, billing reminder, event notice, seasonal message, staff spotlight

---

### Adding a New Child

**Path:** Admin → Children → Add Child
- Complete all required fields: name, DOB, start date, classroom assignment, authorized pickup list, dietary restrictions, allergies, emergency contacts
- Upload immunization records to the child's document folder
- Verify the child appears correctly in roll call before first day

---

### Tadpoles Daily Documentation Standard (Reminder)

Every child, every day, by 3:00 PM:
- **2 staged photos** (clear, intentional, child actively engaged)
- **2 sentences** describing what the child did, learned, or experienced
- Good example: *"Jaylen built a three-story tower with the unit blocks today and counted the levels out loud — 'one, two, free!' During art, he used blue and yellow paint to make green, then announced 'I made a new color!'"*

sections.title: Director Quick-Start Checklist
learningObjectives: Have a single-page daily and weekly checklist to reference when overwhelmed
Know the absolute non-negotiables that must happen every day without exception
Use this section as a self-audit tool on tough days
sections.content:
## When You're Overwhelmed — Start Here

Some days everything happens at once. Use this checklist to ensure the non-negotiables happen even on the hardest days.

---

### Daily Non-Negotiables (Every Day, No Exceptions)

- [ ] Be visible at drop-off (7:30–9:00 AM) — on the floor, not in your office
- [ ] Parent AM Tadpoles message sent by **9:15 AM**
- [ ] Morning Message printed and distributed to all classrooms (with staff signatures)
- [ ] Bleach bottles made fresh and labeled; distributed to classrooms
- [ ] Lunch / closing coverage confirmed in ProCare
- [ ] Tadpoles notes reviewed — all children documented by 3:00 PM
- [ ] Be visible at pick-up (3:00–6:00 PM) — lobby, not office
- [ ] DOR submitted before you leave — **no exceptions**

---

### Weekly Non-Negotiables

- [ ] Roll Call sheets distributed Monday morning
- [ ] Immunization report checked (Tuesday)
- [ ] Maintenance list to robhichens84@gmail.com by **Wednesday 12:00 PM**
- [ ] Overtime report reviewed Wednesday
- [ ] Director Packet complete and submitted **Thursday**
- [ ] Leadership meeting attended **Thursday**
- [ ] Lesson plans reviewed (all submitted by Thursday)
- [ ] Paychecks distributed **Thursday after 5 PM**
- [ ] DOR and WOR submitted by **Friday 3:00 PM** (not 5 PM)
- [ ] All labor entered in ProCare by **Friday 5:00 PM**

---

### Monthly Non-Negotiables

- [ ] Late fees applied — **Day 1**
- [ ] Voicemail greeting changed — **Day 1**
- [ ] Fire drill conducted and documented — **Day 1 or first business day**
- [ ] Bad debt report run; balances contacted — **Day 5**
- [ ] Brivo access updated (new starts / departures) — **Day 6**
- [ ] Billing audit reports run — **Day 20**
- [ ] Tuition posted — **Day 23**
- [ ] Enhancement fees verified on all accounts — **before Day 27**
- [ ] **BILLING DAY (TE auto-charge runs) — Day 27**
- [ ] KPI scorecard submitted — **Day 29**

---

### Emergency Fast Reference

| Situation | First Call |
|-----------|------------|
| Fire / Medical / Intruder | **911** |
| Licensing walk-in | Ask for card → Text admin → Walk with inspector |
| Snow day question | Text admin immediately — do not post closures independently |
| Irate parent | Get them off the lobby floor → Listen → Acknowledge → 1-hour resolution commitment |
| Unauthorized vehicle | Do not approach → Note plate → Call 911 if lingers → Notify admin |
| Billing question you can't answer | "Let me research that and call you back within the hour" → Consult ProCare → Call back |
| Staff no-call no-show | Cover ratio first → Text on Group Me → If no response by start time, call → Document |

---

### You Are Equipped

You've completed all seven modules of the Bright Beginnings Director Training Program. You have the operations knowledge, the people management framework, the compliance understanding, and the community-building skills to lead a great center.

This Reference Library stays with you. Come back to any section whenever you need a quick answer. Birdie is always available for questions that don't fit neatly into a reference guide.

> *Trust the training. Trust the system. Trust yourself.*
> 
> — Bright Beginnings Leadership Team
